evidence: Build/check/test clean on the merged main under the pinned toolchain (all six package suites zero-fail). scan-comment-citations.ts reports totalInstances 0 across all 7 packages; parity-check exits 0 with all 83 writers classified. The iterate-to-zero loop ran three adversarial audit rounds: round 1 confirmed all 9 rewritten sites preserve their semantic content (including the 'real 30' antecedent surviving the generic-placeholder rewrite) and surfaced one missed sibling -- a decision-id citation in a gate-emitted unexposed-writer reason string, invisible to the comment scanner; round 2 confirmed that fix but found its residual phrase swapped the citation for an unanchored internal planning term; round 3 passed with zero findings (the reason string is self-sufficient, accurate against the codebase -- no shipped clone surface contradicts 'planned' -- and the CHANGELOG pair is coherent and id-free). Regression sweep confirmed nothing consumed the old strings programmatically (tests assert error names and commit_sha only, never the message texts).

criteria_results.criterion: A fresh enumeration of non-test packages/*/src comments finds no internal tracker-ID citations outside the deliberately-excluded illustrative examples the FGAP-136 closure names
Generated work-order commit messages carry no internal tracker IDs -- the message template names the work-order id and plain-English purpose only
The rewritten comments preserve each site's semantic content in code-valid plain English
build, check, and full test suite exit 0
